Name: Venonat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.

Name: Venomoth
Effects: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card from his or her hand during his or her next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that card has no effect. (Your opponent still discards that card.)
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The scales it scatters will paralyze anyone who touches them, making that person unable to stand.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won’t miss prey—even those behind it.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It prefers to battle by biting apart foes’ heads instantly while flying by at high speed.

Name: Sewaddle
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

Name: Swadloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

Name: Leavanny
Effects: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no Pokémon Tools attached to them. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Heal 20 damage from each of your [G] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth.

Name: Karrablast
Flavor-Text: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

Name: Fletchinder
Flavor-Text: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.

Name: Talonflame
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is increased by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: In the fever of an exciting battle, it showers embers from the gaps between its feathers and takes to the air.

Name: Litleo
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body.

Name: Pyroar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

Name: Krabby
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.

Name: Kingler
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

Name: Totodile
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is small but rough and tough. It won’t hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

Name: Croconaw
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

Name: Feraligatr
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

Name: Finneon
Flavor-Text: The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

Name: Lumineon
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It lives on the deep-sea floor. It attracts prey by flashing the patterns on its four tail fins.

Name: Frillish
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: They paralyze prey with poison, then drag them down to their lairs, five miles below the surface.

Name: Jellicent
Effects: Attach 3 Energy cards from your opponent’s discard pile to his or her Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is mostly seawater. It’s said there’s a castle of ships Jellicent have sunk on the seafloor.

Name: Alomomola
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.

Name: Manectric-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Manectric-EX
Effects: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: The Defending Pokémon’s Weakness is now [L] until the end of your next turn. (The amount of Weakness doesn’t change.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

Name: Joltik
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Pokémon in your discard pile that have the Night March attack.
Flavor-Text: Since it can’t generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.

Name: Galvantula
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with your opponent’s Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: They employ an electrically charged web to trap their prey. While it is immobilized by shock, they leisurely consume it.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Discard as many [L] Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
Flavor-Text: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

Name: Zubat
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Even though it has no eyes, it can sense obstacles using ultrasonic waves it emits from it mouth.

Name: Golbat
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Once it bites, it will not stop draining energy from the victim even if it gets too heavy to fly.

Name: Crobat
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It flies so silently through the dark on its four wings that it may not be noticed even when nearby.

Name: Gengar-EX
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Gengar-EX
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wobbuffet
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, each Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities (except for [P] Pokémon).
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.

Name: Gulpin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach compromises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.

Name: Swalot
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If, before doing damage, your opponent’s Active Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

Name: Munna
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears before people and Pokémon who are having nightmares and eats those dreams.

Name: Musharna
Effects: Shuffle 3 cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon.

Name: Litwick
Flavor-Text: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 20 damage times the number of Pokémon in your discard pile that have the Night March attack.
Flavor-Text: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: Put 6 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Being consumed in Chandelure’s flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

Name: Pumpkaboo
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Pokémon in your discard pile that have the Night March attack.
Flavor-Text: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

Name: Gourgeist
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [G] Energy attached to it, its maximum HP is 200.
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: Your opponent can’t attach Energy from his or her hand to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

Name: Roggenrola
Flavor-Text: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

Name: Boldore
Effects: Discard a [F] Energy attached to this Pokémon and heal 50 damage from it.
Flavor-Text: Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.

Name: Gigalith
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP, any damage done to this Pokémon by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 50 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Overdrive Smash attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The solar rays it absorbs are processed in its energy core and fired as a ball of light.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It hides any shiny object it finds in a secret location. Murkrow and Meowth loot one another’s stashes.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If one utters a deep cry, many Murkrow gather quickly. For this, it is called “Summoner of Night.”

Name: Poochyena
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

Name: Mightyena
Flavor-Text: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. It is bound to a fissure in an odd keystone.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its cute act is a ruse. When victims let down their guard, they find their items taken. It attacks with sharp claws.

Name: Liepard
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 60 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Their beautiful form comes from the muscles they have developed. They run silently in the night.

Name: Malamar-EX
Ability-Effect: When you attach an Energy from your hand to this Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Discard a [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After nesting in bramble bushes, the wings of its chicks grow hard from scratches by thorns.

Name: Bronzor
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [M] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

Name: Dialga-EX
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard 2 [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Heatran
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Escavalier
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

Name: Aegislash-EX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has Special Energy attached to it.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Klefki
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

Name: Florges-EX
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 20 damage times the number of [G] Pokémon and [Y] Pokémon you have in play.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Swirlix
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and as sticky as cotton candy.

Name: Slurpuff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, draw 1 more card.
Effects: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

Name: Diancie
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Heal 30 damage from each of your [Y] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

Name: Deino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [D] Energy card from your discard pile to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Their heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

Name: Goomy
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn’t dry out.

Name: Sliggoo
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can’t see anything.

Name: Goodra
Ability-Effect: Each player can’t attach any Pokémon Tool cards from his or her hand to any of his or her Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

Name: Spearow
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Very protective of its territory, it flaps its short wings busily to dart around at high speed.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land or rest.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
If you don’t have exactly 7 cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It is said to deliver happiness. Being compassionate, it shares its egg with injured people.

Name: Blissey
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on each of your Benched Pokémon.
You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

Name: Girafarig
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [P] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: While it sleeps, the head of its tail keeps watch. The tail doesn’t need to sleep.

Name: Whismur
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It usually murmurs but starts crying loudly if it senses danger. It stops when its ear covers are shut.

Name: Loudred
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shouts loudly by inhaling air, and then using its well-developed stomach muscles to exhale.

Name: Exploud
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its howls can be heard over six miles away. It emits all sorts of noises from the ports on its body.

Name: Regigigas
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

Name: Bunnelby
Flavor-Text: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites.

Name: Fletchling
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

Name: Furfrou
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

Name: AZ
Description: Put 1 of your Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Battle Compressor Team Flare Gear
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and discard them. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Dimension Valley
Description: Each [P] Pokémon’s attacks (both yours and your opponent’s) cost [C] less.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Enhanced Hammer
Description: Discard a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Gengar Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gengar-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Hand Scope
Description: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Head Ringer Team Flare Hyper Gear
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to cost [C] more.When this card is removed from a Pokémon for any reason, put this card in its owner’s discard pile.

Name: Jamming Net Team Flare Hyper Gear
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 less damage to all Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)When this card is removed from a Pokémon for any reason, put this card in its owner’s discard pile.

Name: Lysandre’s Trump Card
Description: Each player shuffles all cards in his or her discard pile into his or her deck (except for Lysandre’s Trump Card).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Manectric Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Manectric-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Professor Sycamore
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Robo Substitute Team Flare Gear
Description: Play this card as if it were a 30 HP [C] Basic Pokémon. At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may discard this card from play.This card can’t retreat. If this card is Knocked Out, your opponent can’t take any Prize Cards for it.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Roller Skates
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, draw 3 cards.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Shauna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Steel Shelter
Description: Each [M] Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) can’t be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Target Whistle Team Flare Gear
Description: Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent’s discard pile onto his or her Bench.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Tierno
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Trick Coin
Description: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack of the Pokémon this card is attached to, you may ignore all effects of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. (You may only use effects that let you flip coins again, including effects from other cards, once during your turn.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: VS Seeker
Description: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Xerosic
Description: Choose a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card attached to a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent’s) and discard it.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Double Colorless Energy
Description: Double Colorless Energy provides [C][C] Energy.

Name: Mystery Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [P] Pokémon. This card provides [P] Energy only while this card is attached to a [P] Pokémon.The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is [C][C] less.(If this card is attached to anything other than a [P] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Manectric-EX
Move-Damage: 20 damage damage. This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gengar-EX
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Malamar-EX
Ability-Effect: When you attach an Energy from your hand to this Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Florges-EX
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 20 damage times the number of [G] Pokémon and [Y] Pokémon you have in play.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: AZ
Description: Put 1 of your Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lysandre’s Trump Card
Description: Each player shuffles all cards in his or her discard pile into his or her deck (except for Lysandre’s Trump Card).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Xerosic
Description: Choose a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card attached to a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent’s) and discard it.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: M Manectric-EX
Effects: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Gengar-EX
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dialga-EX
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard 2 [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Aegislash-EX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has Special Energy attached to it.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
